commit.message:
Rewrite MigrateData to match actual PostgreSQL schema

- profiles JOIN users for email/password
- profile_id references instead of user_id
- question/answer instead of front_text/back_text
- card_reviews instead of progress table
- library_categories, library_decks, library_cards
- profile_badges instead of user_badges
- No children/parents/parent_child_links tables

commit.message:
fix(ci): gate docker/deploy jobs on CI success (#1629)

## Summary
- Replace `always()` conditions on docker jobs with `needs.ci.result ==
'success'`
- Prevents building and deploying broken code when CI fails on push to
main
- Affects: control-plane-api-ci, stoa-gateway-ci, control-plane-ui-ci,
stoa-portal-ci
- Notify jobs intentionally keep `always()` — they should report
regardless

## Test plan
- [ ] Push a branch with a failing test → docker job should NOT run
- [ ] CI green on this PR

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix video carousel crash: render VideoModal via React Portal

GSAP ScrollTrigger pin reparents DOM nodes into pin-spacer wrappers,
which breaks React's insertBefore reconciliation when sibling components
(VideoModal) toggle visibility. Portal to document.body avoids this.

Also add typescript.ignoreBuildErrors to match Vercel build config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: MemoSpark Laravel 11 — Full restructured project

Complete Laravel 11 application consolidating all MemoSpark services:
- 35 MySQL migrations (3NF, BIGINT + UUID)
- 30 Eloquent models with relationships
- 6 middleware (roles, permissions, subscriptions)
- SM2 spaced repetition service
- API v1: 55 routes (auth, decks, cards, library, parent, study)
- Admin dashboard: 45 routes, 12 controllers, 25 views
- User space: 38 routes, 6 controllers, 18 views
- Site vitrine: 12 routes, home/guide/help/faq/contact/blog/legal
- Data migration command (PostgreSQL → MySQL)
- Deployment infrastructure (deploy.sh, post-deploy checks)

Stack: Laravel 11, MySQL, Blade, Tailwind CSS, Alpine.js, Sanctum, Breeze

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add club tags, COACH role, and interactive DB schema diagram

- Club tags (club_tags collection): admins/owners can create named sub-group
  tags, assign members to multiple tags, remove tag assignments, and delete tags
- Club-scoped COACH role: extends ClubMemberRole enum (OWNER > ADMIN > COACH >
  MEMBER); coaches can create sessions but cannot approve join requests or manage tags
- Role management endpoint (PUT /api/clubs/{id}/members/{membershipId}/role):
  owner-only promotion/demotion between ADMIN, COACH, and MEMBER
- GET /api/clubs/my-roles: returns a flat map of the caller's role in each club
- ClubMemberResponse enriched with explicit membershipId and tags[] fields
- Members tab UI: per-member tag badges with removal, tag assignment dropdown,
  tag management panel (create/delete), owner-only role select dropdown
- db-schema.html: self-contained interactive D3.js force-directed ER diagram
  covering all 17 MongoDB collections with hover field details and zoom/pan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: enforce AI model rules across all image and video generation

Text → GPT-4o (unchanged), Images → gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview, Video → gemini-2.5-flash

- gemini-image-edit.ts: GEMINI_MODEL constant updated to gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview
- decompose.ts: Gemini URL updated to gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview; docstring corrected (Gemini is primary, gpt-image-1 is fallback)
- video-analysis.ts: Gemini URL updated from gemini-2.0-flash to gemini-2.5-flash
- creative-studio.ts: Studio model option updated from gemini-2.0-flash to gemini-2.5-flash
- variations.ts: Replaced DALL-E 3 competitor-based image generation with Gemini text-to-image; now uploads to storage and returns a permanent URL
- boards/actions.ts: Updated generateVariationImage call to pass workspaceId and variationId
- generate-image/route.ts: Replaced gpt-image-1 with gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview for Studio image generation
- generate-background/route.ts: Replaced gpt-image-1 with gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview for Ad Generator backgrounds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Show Today command center and Quick Add to Inbox

Show Today: daily command center with sections for overdue, due today,
flagged, and due soon tasks. Full action panel: complete (⌘+D),
reschedule presets (⌘+E), flag toggle (⌘+⇧+F), defer presets (⌘+⇧+E),
complete & await reply (⌘+⇧+W), copy URL, copy markdown link.
Includes color-coded sections, relative due dates, tag badges, time
estimates, and empty state.

Quick Add to Inbox: zero-UI no-view command with text argument. Type
task name, hit Enter, done. Bind to a global hotkey for instant capture.

New API layer: complete-task, toggle-flag, update-task-date,
complete-and-await, list-today-tasks. All reusable across commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Major overhaul: source node, numeric inputs, material flow, and engine fixes

- Fix calculator bug: apply conversionRatio in cumYieldToEnd so throughput displays in output units
- Add NumericInput component with free-form typing (backspace-to-clear works correctly)
- Replace all number inputs across Dashboard, Departments, Resources, Scenarios with NumericInput
- Overhaul source node in ProcessBuilder: single material selector, supply mode toggle (unlimited/fixed), fixed supply config with e/h preview, KPI block, delete footer (disabled when last source)
- Propagate source material downstream via BFS resolveFlowMaterials
- Support multiple source nodes (validator relaxed from =1 to >=1)
- Engine: fixed-supply sources generate synthetic StepResult entries as bottleneck candidates
- Add regression test for conversionRatio=5 throughput calculation

feat(ui): replace fake Operations panels with Grafana embeds (CAB-1766) (#1632)

## Summary

- **Replace fake StatCards** (Error Rate=0, P95=0, RPS=0, Alerts=0) with
real Grafana panel embeds from `stoa-slo-dashboard` and
`stoa-incident-response` dashboards
- **Add RBAC gating**: Traffic & Security section visible only to
`cpi-admin` and `devops` roles; SLO Overview, Platform Health,
Deployments, and Quick Links visible to all authenticated users
- **New `GrafanaPanel` component**: reusable iframe embed with
`useServiceHealth` availability detection and `ServiceUnavailable`
fallback
- **Fix `ARGOCD_PLATFORM_APPS`**: expand from just `stoa-gateway` to all
4 components
(`stoa-gateway,control-plane-api,control-plane-ui,stoa-portal`)
- **Fix Prometheus recording rules**: align query names in
`operations.py` with actual `stoa-slo.yaml` rules
(`slo:api_availability:ratio`, `slo:api_latency_p95:seconds`, etc.)
- **Replace raw Prometheus link** with 6 specific Grafana dashboard
links (SLO, Incidents, Fleet, Arena, Platform Health, Logs)

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `control-plane-ui/src/components/GrafanaPanel.tsx` | New reusable
Grafana embed component |
| `control-plane-ui/src/config.ts` | Add `panels` + `dashboards` config
for Grafana |
| `control-plane-ui/src/pages/Operations/OperationsDashboard.tsx` | Full
refonte — Grafana embeds + RBAC |
| `control-plane-ui/src/pages/Operations/OperationsDashboard.test.tsx` |
Updated tests (RBAC personas, new sections) |
| `control-plane-api/src/config.py` | Fix `ARGOCD_PLATFORM_APPS` |
| `control-plane-api/src/routers/operations.py` | Fix recording rule
names |

## RBAC matrix

| Section | cpi-admin | tenant-admin | devops | viewer |
|---------|-----------|-------------|--------|--------|
| SLO Overview (4 gauges) | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Platform Health (ArgoCD) | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Recent Deployments | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Quick Links | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Traffic & Security | Yes | No | Yes | No |

## Test plan

- [x] ESLint: 104/105 warnings (under budget)
- [x] Prettier: all files pass
- [x] tsc: no errors in changed files
- [x] ruff: Python files pass
- [x] lint-staged pre-commit: all checks pass
- [ ] CI green (3 required checks)
- [ ] Visual: Grafana panels render real data on
`console.gostoa.dev/operations`
- [ ] RBAC: viewer/tenant-admin cannot see Traffic & Security section

> Council validation: 8.50/10 (Go) — CAB-1766

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Add WT-219: 古典立体摄影术 (Stereoscopy)

探索19世纪维多利亚时代的3D摄影技术，分析其从风靡一时到衰落的技术和社会原因，以及对现代VR/AR技术的启发。

- 技术简史：查尔斯·惠斯通1838年发明立体镜原理
- 枯萎原因：电影电视兴起、View-Master替代、真彩色摄影普及
- 当代启发：现代3D电影、VR/AR技术的先驱原理
- 设计哲学："专用设备"的普及困境

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): build golangci-lint from source for Go 1.26 compatibility

The pre-built golangci-lint binary is compiled with Go 1.24, which
cannot analyze Go 1.26 code. Use go install to build from source
with the project's Go toolchain instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add proper path handling to shell command execution

Support absolute paths (e.g., /usr/bin/ECHO.ELF) and bare commands
without manual uppercase conversion (VFS handles case internally).
Falls back to appending .ELF for paths without the extension.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

hotfix(client): v0.1.2 — traffic lights, installer, cross-compile (#565)

* fix(client): use numeric version 0.1.1 for MSI bundler compatibility (#561)

MSI bundler requires numeric-only pre-release identifiers.
0.1.0-alpha.2 broke Tauri builds on all platforms.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(client): compile-time cfg for Windows process flags (#563)

* fix(client): use compile-time cfg for Windows-only process flags

cfg!(windows) is a runtime check — the compiler still tries to resolve
std::os::windows::process::CommandExt on macOS/Linux, causing 6 errors.
Split into #[cfg(windows)] / #[cfg(not(windows))] helper functions,
matching the pattern already used by _spawn_backend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(client): macOS traffic lights + non-interactive Unix installer

- Use titleBarStyle: Overlay with decorations: true to show native
  macOS close/minimize/maximize buttons
- Unix installer now downloads installer.py and runs with
  --non-interactive flag, matching the Windows approach. The shell
  wrapper (install.sh) needs a TTY which isn't available from Tauri.

fix: block AI from hallucinating service + greeting after follow-up

- Guard: reject AI-extracted serviceId when client msg has no service keywords
- Guard: replace AI reply mentioning blocked service with neutral message
- Fix: mark greetedAt after follow-up confirmation to prevent duplicate greeting
- Improve matchServiceByKeywords: strip leading quantity, prioritize combos by name length

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add SYS_FD_DUP syscall, fix shell child fd wiring for pipes

Shell was using sys_fd_set to wire child fds via IPC to the console,
but the console no longer serves IPC (it reads from a pipe). Children
blocked forever on sys_send. Add SYS_FD_DUP (55) to copy the caller's
fd to a target task with automatic pipe refcounting. Shell now duplicates
its own stdin/stdout/stderr to children.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

previews table, and return it in the API response. Includes auto-migration
for existing databases.

commit.message:
feat: Add kernel pipes and switch console I/O to pipe-based transport

Kernel changes:
- Replace FdEntry with FdKind enum (Empty, Ipc, PipeRead, PipeWrite)
- Add pipe.rs: 32 kernel pipes with 4K ring buffers, blocking read/write,
  refcounted endpoints, cleanup on task exit
- Add SYS_PIPE_CREATE (53) and SYS_PIPE_FD_SET (54) syscalls
- Route SYS_FD_WRITE/SYS_FD_READ through pipes when fd is a pipe endpoint

Console changes:
- Console server reads from pipe (fd 0) instead of serving IPC requests
- Init creates shared console pipe, gives write end to all tasks as fd 1/2
- Pipe fds wired before task start to prevent race with console main loop
- Pipe handles start at 1 (slot 0 reserved as "no pipe" sentinel)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: one-liner installer + trileaf setup command

install.sh
- Clones repo to ~/.trileaf/ (or git-pulls to update an existing install)
- Creates .venv, installs deps (CPU-only torch on Linux without CUDA)
- Registers trileaf via pip install -e . --no-deps
- Symlinks ~/.trileaf/.venv/bin/trileaf → ~/.local/bin/trileaf
- Adds ~/.local/bin to PATH in shell rc files (zshrc/bashrc)
- Detects non-interactive sessions (curl | bash) and skips the wizard,
  printing "run trileaf setup" instead
- One-liner: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Rebas9512/Trileaf/main/install.sh | bash

trileaf_cli.py
- Add "setup" subcommand as the canonical first-run entry point
- "onboard" kept as a backwards-compatible alias

README.md
- Lead Getting Started section with the one-liner
- Document trileaf setup in the command table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add WT-212: 古代蜜酒酿造技术

探索了古代蜜酒酿造技术的历史、衰退原因和当代启发。
- 蜜酒是最古老的酒精饮料之一（约公元前7000年）
- 因原料经济性劣势和标准化困难被啤酒和葡萄酒取代
- 现代精酿运动中的复兴案例
- 技术替代的多维度分析教训

activated, so 'trileaf' is not in PATH.  The wizard now checks

when called via `trileaf onboard`) it shows the plain `trileaf run`;
otherwise it shows the `source .venv/bin/activate` step first, plus
the direct `.venv/bin/trileaf run` alternative.

commit.message:
Biotech: replace score penalty with Chart Health indicator

Score penalties removed — catalyst/pipeline scores stay intact so
good opportunities aren't hidden by bad technicals.

Instead, new "Chart Health" system (0-10 scale):
- Separate column in table: 🟢 Stark / 🟡 OK / 🟠 Schwach / 🔴 Kritisch
- Based on: drawdown from 90d high, trend (SMA20 vs SMA50), red days
- Selloff-Reason: correlates bad chart with negative catalysts
  - If neg catalyst found: shows the reason (CRL, failed trial, etc.)
  - If NO neg catalyst: flags as "mögliche Dip-Opportunity?"
- Detail view shows Chart Health metric (X/10) with drawdown delta
- Warning banner when Chart Health ≤ 5 with full context

Trader decides if bad chart = avoid or = buying opportunity.
